University of Arizona · Medical Imaging AI

Physics meets AI
in medical imaging.

Advancing Medical Imaging AI Through Virtual Trials and Computational Intelligence

Explore Research Join the Lab

Open Positions Available
↓

Our Mission

Bridging simulation and AI to advance radiology

The Tushar Lab develops physics-based simulation frameworks and deep learning methods to bridge the gap between data scarcity and clinical AI validation in medical imaging. We combine virtual imaging trials, digital human phantoms, and foundation models to create robust, trustworthy AI systems for radiology.

Read more about our research →

Research

What we work on

🫁

Virtual Imaging Trials

Physics-based simulation of CT imaging systems using digital human phantoms for in silico clinical evaluation.

Virtual Lung Screening Trial (VLST)SYN-LUNGS
→
🧠

Medical Imaging AI

Foundation models and weakly-supervised deep learning for robust, data-efficient radiology AI.

NodMAISIDukeSeg
→
📊

Image Quality & Evaluation

Objective, task-based frameworks for quantifying CT image quality and AI model performance.

CPU-Index FrameworkTask-Based Image Quality
→
⚙️

Generative AI for Imaging

Synthetic data generation for augmenting scarce clinical datasets and enabling robust model training.

SYN-LUNGS SynthesisPathology-Conditioned CT Generation
→
All Research Areas →

Highlights

Flagship projects

Selected ongoing research shaping the future of in silico medical imaging evaluation.

01

Virtual Lung Screening Trial (VLST)

An end-to-end virtual clinical trial framework simulating patient anatomy, CT acquisition, and AI-based nodule detection — enabling large-scale, radiation-free protocol evaluation.

CT Simulation Digital Phantoms Lung Cancer
02

NodMAISI — Nodule Multi-scale AI

A multi-scale foundation model for pulmonary nodule detection and characterization in low-dose and standard-dose CT, achieving robust performance with minimal labeled data.

Foundation Models Weakly Supervised Nodule Detection
03

CPU-Index Framework

A comprehensive, task-based CT protocol utility index that quantifies image quality in terms of clinically meaningful diagnostic tasks — bridging physics and clinical performance.

Image Quality CT Protocol Evaluation

Lab News

Latest updates

Announcement January 15, 2025

Tushar Lab Officially Launches at the University of Arizona

We are excited to announce the official launch of the Tushar Lab at the University of Arizona's Department of Medical Imaging. The lab will focus on virtual imaging trials and deep learning for radiology AI.

Publication November 10, 2024

VLST Paper Accepted in Medical Physics

Our paper on the Virtual Lung Screening Trial framework has been accepted for publication in Medical Physics journal.

Conference December 1, 2024

Lab Presents at RSNA 2024

Dr. Tushar presented research on virtual imaging trials and AI-based nodule detection at the Radiological Society of North America Annual Meeting in Chicago.

All News →
